patterns: how long does a class last
responses: CSU Global courses are 8 weeks long. Most students take one course at a time, letting them focus on a single subject while balancing work and life responsibilities.
Each course at CSU Global is 8 weeks. The accelerated format lets you move through your degree without waiting for a traditional semester.

patterns: when is the next start date
responses: CSU Global offers monthly start dates — classes begin every four weeks, so you don't have to wait for a traditional fall or spring semester to get started.
With monthly start dates, you can begin almost any month. Classes begin every four weeks throughout the academic year.
CSU Global operates on three 16-week trimesters: Fall, Winter, and Spring. Each trimester contains two 8-week terms (A/B or C/D), giving you multiple entry points throughout the year.
The academic year has three trimesters — Fall, Winter, and Spring — each 16 weeks long with two 8-week terms inside.

patterns: term a term b term c term d
responses: Each trimester has four terms: A, B, C, and D. Burgundy track students take classes in A and C terms; Gold track students take classes in B and D terms. Each term is 8 weeks long.
Terms A, B, C, and D are the 8-week slots within each trimester. Your Gold or Burgundy track determines which terms you're enrolled in.

The Burgundy Track means your courses start in A and C terms — the first 8-week slot in each trimester. Your track is set by when you first enrolled at CSU Global. Download the 2025-2026 Burgundy calendar: https://info.csuglobal.edu/2025-2026-burgundy-track
Burgundy Track students begin classes in A or C terms. Both Gold and Burgundy follow the same 3-trimester structure; the only difference is the starting term within each trimester.
The Gold Track means your courses start in B and D terms — the second 8-week slot in each trimester. Download the 2025-2026 Gold calendar: https://info.csuglobal.edu/2025-2026-gold-track
Gold Track students begin classes in B or D terms. It gives you a predictable schedule with built-in breaks without sitting out a full trimester.

patterns: what is the difference between gold and burgundy
responses: The only difference between Gold and Burgundy tracks is when your classes start within each trimester. Burgundy students start in A/C terms; Gold students start in B/D terms. Both follow the same 3-trimester, 8-week structure. Your track is assigned based on when you first enrolled.
Gold and Burgundy are two scheduling tracks — same structure, different start slots. Burgundy: A and C terms. Gold: B and D terms. This gives everyone built-in breaks and a predictable calendar.

patterns: do i take one course at a time
responses: Most CSU Global students take one 8-week course at a time. This lets you focus on one subject while managing work and personal commitments — building momentum without feeling overwhelmed.
CSU Global is designed for one course at a time. The focused 8-week format keeps your schedule manageable and your learning effective.

patterns: what is a student success counselor
responses: Every CSU Global student is matched with a dedicated Student Success Counselor. Your counselor provides personalized support from your first class through graduation, helping with degree planning, goal setting, course registration, policy questions, and connecting you to resources like tutoring, the library, and career services.

Your Student Success Counselor is your single point of contact at CSU Global. They're available by phone, email, and live chat to help with educational plans, registration, instructor relationships, and accessing campus resources. Contact: 877-900-2655 | student.success@csuglobal.edu
CSU Global offers tutoring help in over 250 subject areas. When you get stuck on an assignment, help is just a click away through your student success counselor or the academic support portal.
Tutoring is available to all CSU Global students in 250+ subjects. Visit: https://csuglobal.edu/student-success/academic-support/tutoring

The CSU Global Writing Center lets you submit any academic writing — from a single paragraph to a full paper — and receive detailed feedback from a writing expert. It helps improve your paper and build long-term writing skills.
Submit your academic writing to the Writing Center for expert feedback. Available to all students through the academic support resources.

CSU Global's Career Development Services are free for students and alumni. Services include:
• Career coaching (1-on-1 sessions with an expert)
• Resume and cover letter help (reviewed by a Certified Resume Writer)
• LinkedIn profile guidance
• Interview preparation and mock interviews
• Job and internship search support
• Global Connect networking platform
Visit: https://csuglobal.edu/student-success/career-development

CSU Global's Career Services team can help you build and review your resume. You'll get access to sample resumes, ATS (Applicant Tracking System) tips, and a review by staff trained by a Certified Professional Resume Writer — all free for students and alumni.
Resume reviews and cover letter help are available free through CSU Global Career Services. The team is trained by a Certified Professional Resume Writer.

CSU Global Career Services offers interview tips, common interview questions, suggested questions to ask employers, and 50-minute mock interviews with an interview expert — all free for students and alumni.
You can schedule a mock interview with an interview expert through CSU Global's Career Services. They also provide tips, common questions, and strategies to help you succeed.

CSU Global's Disability and Accessibility Services provide individualized assistance for students with unique needs. The office is committed to helping all students achieve their educational goals. Visit: https://disabilityservices.csuglobal.edu/
CSU Global offers Disability and Accessibility Services designed to support every student. Contact the office at https://disabilityservices.csuglobal.edu/ for accommodations and assistance.
CSU Global offers on-demand IT Support available any time of day so you can complete coursework on your schedule. Call: 877-512-1974
On-demand tech support is available 24/7 at CSU Global. Phone: 877-512-1974. Your student success counselor can also connect you if needed.

CSU Global has an extensive online library with a dedicated librarian to help you navigate research and resources. Access it through your student portal or ask your student success counselor to connect you.
The CSU Global library offers research support with an online librarian available to advise you. Access is through the student portal.

The CSU Global Office of the Registrar handles student records and transcripts.
• Phone: 800-462-7845 x5
• Email: registrar@csuglobal.edu
• Transcript orders: https://csuglobal.edu/resources/student-services/ordering-transcripts
• Fax: 720-302-8506

CSU Global's Student Financial Services office handles financial aid, billing, and military benefits.
• Main: 855-212-6700
• Financial Aid (x3): financialaid@csuglobal.edu
• Military Benefits (x1): military.benefits@csuglobal.edu
• Student Accounts/Billing (x2): studentaccounts@csuglobal.edu
Visit: https://csuglobal.edu/tuition-aid/financial-aid

CSU Global offers a tuition guarantee — your rate stays the same from your first class to your last. CSU Global charges $0 in student fees: no out-of-state fees, athletic fees, per-credit fees, or hidden charges. It's ranked among the most affordable online universities by U.S. News & World Report. For current rates visit: https://csuglobal.edu/tuition-aid/tuition-cost

Once you enroll, CSU Global locks in your tuition rate for your entire program. There are no student fees of any kind — no out-of-state, athletic, or per-credit fees. Visit https://csuglobal.edu/tuition-aid/tuition-cost for current tuition rates.

Apply at: https://myapplication.csuglobal.edu/apply
There is a $25 application fee, but fee waivers are sometimes available.
To apply to CSU Global, visit https://myapplication.csuglobal.edu/apply. Enrollment Counselors are available to help — call (800) 462-7845 or email enroll@csuglobal.edu.

CSU Global offers accredited online bachelor's degrees. Undergraduate admissions information is at: https://csuglobal.edu/admissions/undergraduate-students/

Apply at: https://myapplication.csuglobal.edu/apply

CSU Global offers online graduate programs in many fields. Graduate admissions requirements are at: https://csuglobal.edu/admissions/graduate-students/

CSU Global accepts as much transfer credit as possible to save you time and money. Transfer admissions info: https://csuglobal.edu/admissions/transfer-students/

Transferring from a Colorado Community College? See: https://csuglobal.edu/blog/transferring-from-a-colorado-community-college-to-csu-global-what-you-need-to-know
CSU Global welcomes students from around the world to earn a fully accredited degree from an American university. International admissions: https://csuglobal.edu/admissions/international-students/

CSU Global offers:

📘 Bachelor's Degrees: Accounting, Agile Project Management, Business Management, Computer Science, Criminal Justice, Cybersecurity, Finance, Health Sciences Management, Healthcare Administration, Human Resource Management, Human Services, Information Technology, Interdisciplinary Studies, Legal Studies, Management Information Systems, Marketing, Operations & Supply Chain Management, Organizational Leadership, Project Management.

📙 Certificates and Industry Certification Prep (CompTIA, AWS, Microsoft Azure, ISACA, ISC2, EC-Council, etc.)

Browse all: https://csuglobal.edu/academic-programs/

CSU Global is the first independent, fully-accredited, 100% online state university established in the United States. It is part of the Colorado State University System — alongside CSU Fort Collins and CSU-Pueblo — governed by the same CSU System Board of Governors. Together the three schools serve 52,000 scholars annually with 200,000+ alumni worldwide.

CSU Global is a fully accredited, 100% online public university in the Colorado State University System. Your diploma is conferred by the CSU System Board of Governors and features the CSU Global seal. The university is ranked among the best by U.S. News & World Report. Learn more: https://csuglobal.edu/about

CSU Global is fully accredited and part of the Colorado State University System. Your degree is conferred by the CSU System Board of Governors and recognized by employers nationwide. Accreditation details: https://csuglobal.edu/about/accreditation

CSU Global is 100% online with no fixed schedules. Classes are fully online — as long as you have a WiFi connection, you can learn from anywhere. Programs are built for online excellence from day one, not adapted from a traditional campus.
All CSU Global courses are 100% online with flexible weekly deadlines. You never need to be on a physical campus. Programs use multimedia lectures, ebooks, real-world simulations, and more.

CSU Global faculty hold terminal degrees and have real professional experience in their fields. They are specially trained to teach in a dynamic online environment — not just subject matter experts, but skilled online educators.

Instructors at CSU Global have both terminal degrees and industry experience. They're specifically trained for online teaching, and the curriculum is reviewed and updated annually to match current employer demands.
